latest_entry.title: Chronicle of The Ascension
latest_entry.dek: A stitched overview of the strongest pressures shaping Edhra and Valdris.
latest_entry.chronicle:
By 2026-05-01, the world of Edhra is being pulled most strongly by The Ascension, Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written), and Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written). These arcs are no longer isolated disturbances; they are the pressures around which the current age is organizing itself.

The figures and forces surfacing most often in this span include The Ascension, archive, Tower (as artifact/nexus), Tower (as artifact/nexus). Their repeated intersections suggest that the world is cohering around recurring centers of consequence rather than disconnected adventures.

Recent turning points cluster around tales such as The Wanderer's Final Question, The Cartographer's Daughter, The Deepkin Commander's Reflection, The Wyvern's Hunger Returns. Taken together, they show a setting where personal decisions, factional maneuvers, and larger catastrophes are feeding one another instead of unfolding in separate lanes.
current_state: The Ascension is currently at climax stage.
Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written) is currently at climax stage.
Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written) is currently at climax stage.
Luminous Ascension (accidental vs. written) is currently at climax stage.
ongoing_threads: A full Haiku chronicle synthesis has not yet been produced.

scoring_weights.description: Entities repeating across the current chronicle window matter most.
Recurring across multiple issues outranks a one-day spike.
Entities that intersect many others become chronicle-worthy hubs.
Direct ties to major arcs push entities upward.
Events, characters, and places get slightly higher starting weight because they anchor narrative continuity.

Relics.name: the Tear of the Drowned God
the Tear of the Drowned God
